experiments.description: Baseline SAC+HER on FetchPickAndPlace-v4, train.py unmodified.
experiments.hypothesis: The unchanged baseline establishes a starting success rate within the 30-min single-env time budget; expected to be modest (0-30%) given that OpenAI's HER baselines used 19 MPI processes for hours.
experiments.vlm_feedback_summary: Reach is learned (gripper consistently moves to cube vicinity) but grasping and lifting are NOT — cube never moves, so HER relabeling has no manipulation signal. Most successes come from rare goals near the cube's start.
vlm_failure_modes: no_grasping: gripper hovers/descends onto cube but never closes fingers
no_lifting: arm never raises after touching cube, so mid-air goals are unreachable
alpha_collapses_too_fast: auto-alpha drops from 1.0 to ~0.12 in first 8K steps, killing exploration before grasping is discovered
her_signal_starvation: cube never moves -> all relabeled future goals collapse to cube start position -> HER teaches reach, not manipulate
vlm_suggestions: Add LayerNorm in actor + critic hidden layers (cheap, lit-supported)
Higher UTD ratio (e.g., 4 gradient updates per env step)
Slower alpha collapse: target_entropy = -action_dim/2, or fixed alpha=0.2
Bias exploration noise toward the gripper action dim during warmup
MHER(lambda) n-step relabeling — bigger lift; defer until cheaper levers exhausted
experiments.lesson_learned: Baseline lands at 10% success. The dominant failure is exploration / grasping discovery, not optimization quality: HER cannot help with the manipulation phase if the cube never moves in rollouts. Future experiments should attack either (a) exploration around the gripper dimension or (b) extracting more signal per data point (UTD, normalization), since data quantity is fixed.

experiments.description: Add nn.LayerNorm after each hidden Linear in Actor.trunk and Critic.q1/q2 (before activation). Output heads unchanged.
experiments.hypothesis: LayerNorm in actor + critic stabilizes the critic under SAC's high update count (Bjorck et al. 2021), letting weak grasping signal propagate without being washed out by critic instability. Predicted success_rate to move from 0.10 to 0.15-0.30.
changes_made: Actor.trunk: insert nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim) between each Linear and activation
Critic.q1 and Critic.q2: insert nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim) between each Linear and activation
experiments.vlm_feedback_summary: Hypothesis NOT confirmed: success rate regressed 0.10 -> 0.00. Reach itself degraded (gripper hovers above/beside cube, sometimes retreats away). Alpha collapsed even harder under LN (1.0 -> 0.002 vs baseline 0.12), critic_loss settled higher (~1.2 vs ~0.15), throughput dropped 32%.
vlm_failure_modes: alpha_super_collapse: LN appears to let the squashed-Gaussian achieve target entropy cheaply, so auto-alpha drives alpha to ~0 -> zero exploration bonus
reach_regression: gripper no longer reliably reaches cube vicinity (worse than baseline)
critic_loss_inflation: LN'd critic settles at ~1.2 loss vs baseline ~0.15, suggesting LN shifted the loss landscape unfavorably for sparse-reward Q targets
throughput_hit: 170 -> 115 steps/sec (-32%) under fixed time budget reduces total data by 26%
vlm_suggestions: Attack alpha collapse directly (next): target_entropy = -action_dim/2 = -2 instead of -4
If still bad: fixed alpha = 0.2 (no auto tuning)
Then: gripper-biased exploration noise during warmup
Avoid LN in actor when AUTO_ALPHA=True; possibly safe in critic only — but defer this combo until exploration is fixed
experiments.lesson_learned: LayerNorm + AUTO_ALPHA is a hostile combination on this task: LN normalizes pre-activations, which lets the squashed-Gaussian policy reach the auto-alpha target entropy with very low alpha. Exploration vanishes faster than baseline, and the LN'd critic actually fits worse. Throughput penalty (32% slower) compounds the problem under a fixed wall-clock budget. The exp 1 finding stands: exploration / grasping discovery is the dominant bottleneck, optimization-stability tweaks alone make it worse.

experiments.hypothesis: By raising target_entropy magnitude (sic — see lesson_learned: I had the sign convention BACKWARDS), keep auto-alpha from collapsing so exploration persists past 8K steps. Predicted success_rate 0.10 -> 0.15-0.40 with alpha plateauing >= 0.05.
experiments.vlm_feedback_summary: Hypothesis NOT confirmed and rooted in a sign-convention mistake. Alpha collapsed FURTHER than baseline (0.009 vs 0.12), critic_loss diverged to ~50, gripper sometimes retreats from cube instead of reaching it. Mean_distance is ~baseline (0.209 vs 0.214).
vlm_failure_modes: alpha_super_collapse: alpha 1.0 -> 0.009 (vs baseline 0.12) because I LOWERED H_target instead of raising it
critic_divergence: critic_loss explodes to ~50 by end (vs baseline ~0.15) — Q-values diverge with no entropy regularization to constrain them
policy_retreat: in some eval episodes the gripper retreats upward away from cube — reach itself is mildly destabilized
sign_convention_error: code uses target_entropy = -H_target. -action_dim/2 means H_target=2 (lower), not 8 (higher).
vlm_suggestions: If revisiting this lever: target_entropy = -action_dim*2 = -8 to RAISE H_target
But the more impactful next move is dense reward for training (eval stays sparse)
Dense training reward attacks the actual bottleneck: even tiny accidental cube nudges produce useful reward gradient
If dense reward works, then ablate alpha settings on top
experiments.lesson_learned: Two lessons. (1) SIGN CONVENTION: in this codebase, `target_entropy` field is the NEGATIVE of the entropy target H_target (used in alpha update as log_alpha * (log_prob + target_entropy)). To raise exploration, make it MORE negative. (2) MORE IMPORTANT: even a corrected exploration tweak only delays alpha collapse. Random 4D action exploration almost never produces a coordinated grasp+lift event in 30 min, so HER's achieved_goal trajectory is essentially constant and the manipulation signal never emerges. The next experiment must produce signal even without successful grasping — dense reward for training is the cleanest way to do that.

experiments.description: Dense training reward only (eval sparse): training reward = -||next_achieved_goal - desired_goal||; ReplayBuffer._compute_reward switched to same dense form.
experiments.hypothesis: HER fails because cube doesn't move and all relabeled goals collapse to cube-start. Dense reward gives signal even from tiny accidental cube nudges. Predicted success_rate 0.10 -> 0.20-0.60.
changes_made: Training loop: shaped_reward = -||next_obs.achieved_goal - obs.desired_goal||, used in buffer.add
experiments.vlm_feedback_summary: Hypothesis NOT confirmed: success rate stays at 0. Visual behaviour identical to baseline (gripper reaches cube, no manipulation). Critic loss is LOW (~0.08 — fits dense value well) but alpha collapsed to 0.000 because the bigger Q signal made the entropy bonus negligible by comparison; deterministic policy + flat-per-episode reward (cube doesn't move) => no exploration of manipulation.
vlm_failure_modes: alpha_collapse_to_zero: dense reward amplifies Q magnitude so auto-alpha drives alpha exactly to 0
no_random_bumps: with alpha=0 and deterministic policy, gripper never randomly bumps cube, so dense reward gradient is flat per episode (reward ~ -||cube_start - goal||, constant)
task_collapse_to_reach: policy converges to 'minimize ||grip - cube||' since that's the only thing it can affect under near-deterministic actions
vlm_suggestions: NEXT: combine dense reward with FIXED alpha = 0.2 (AUTO_ALPHA = False). Persistent entropy bonus + dense gradient should jointly fix signal starvation and exploration death.
Backup: gripper-biased OU exploration noise during warmup
If dense+fixed alpha still fails, the deterministic-policy bottleneck may need a temporally-correlated exploration scheme rather than i.i.d. action noise.
experiments.lesson_learned: Dense reward IS the right idea for breaking signal starvation, but it cannot work alone with auto-alpha: the bigger Q magnitude makes auto-alpha collapse alpha to zero, killing the very exploration that would let the policy discover manipulation. Auto-alpha effectively re-balances entropy against Q magnitude, so reward-shape changes have first-order effects on the equilibrium alpha. Solution: pin alpha (AUTO_ALPHA=False) so reward shape and exploration stay decoupled.

experiments.description: 组合实验:dense 训练奖励 + 固定 alpha=0.2(AUTO_ALPHA=False)。同时攻击实验 4 暴露的两个症结 — 信号匮乏(用 dense reward 解决)和 auto-alpha 在 dense reward 下崩到 0(用固定 alpha 解决)。eval 仍然走 evaluate.py 的稀疏奖励,success_rate 指标不受影响。
experiments.hypothesis: 实验 1-4 的累积分析指出:HER 在方块不动时退化为 reach,稀疏奖励无梯度,而 auto-alpha 在 dense 奖励下被驱到 0。把这两个问题用 dense reward + 固定 alpha=0.2 同时解决:dense 提供持续梯度,固定 alpha 保证持续随机探索。预期成功率从 0.10 提升到 0.20-0.60,critic_loss 应当稳定在 0.1 附近,alpha 全程 0.2。
changes_made: 训练循环 buffer.add 的 reward 改为 -||next_achieved_goal - desired_goal||(原来是 env 稀疏 reward)
experiments.vlm_feedback_summary: 假设部分成立但没有突破:训练完全稳定(alpha 全程 0.200,critic_loss ~0.12,actor_loss ~-1.0,无发散),但成功率只与基线相同(0.10)。视觉行为也与基线一样 — 夹爪一致地移到方块附近,从不闭合也从不举起。
vlm_failure_modes: exploration_structure_inadequate:i.i.d 高斯噪声(即使 alpha=0.2 不算小)在 4D 动作空间内难以产生协调的'夹爪到位 + 闭合 + 上举'多步序列
policy_locks_into_reach:dense reward 让 critic 收敛得很干净,但 critic 学到的最优策略在没有任何抓取经验的情况下就是'最小化 grip-cube 距离',mean action 收敛到悬停
her_signal_unchanged:整个 buffer 中 achieved_goal 几乎不动,HER 重标注的 future goal 仍然是方块起点附近的小簇,manipulation 信号永不出现
mean_distance_marginally_worse:0.235 vs 基线 0.214,可能是 dense reward 让 policy 更稳定地'悬停'而不像基线那样有时碰巧把方块挪几毫米
vlm_suggestions: 下一步(实验 6):抓取偏置 warmup — STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING 阶段强制 action[3]=-1(夹爪闭合),并把 warmup 从 1000 提升到 5000。让随机手臂运动配合闭合夹爪,提高 buffer 中方块移动事件密度
若仍不行:OU 时间相关探索噪声替代 i.i.d. 高斯,产生持续性的夹爪指令
或:多分量 dense reward(再加上 -lambda * ||grip-cube|| 项)直接塑造夹爪靠近方块的行为
如果以上都不行:回到 dense + 固定 alpha 这一基础上,叠加 UTD>1 提升每步学习量
experiments.lesson_learned: 干净地证明了一件重要事:在这个任务上,信号(dense reward)和探索量(固定 alpha)同时修复后,仍然无法突破基线 0.10。问题不是不够的随机噪声,也不是不够的训练信号 — 而是探索的*结构*。i.i.d 高斯噪声在 4D 动作空间不会自然产生'多步协调的抓取序列'(夹爪到位 + 闭合 + 上举)。下一实验必须改变探索结构本身,而不是音量。最直接的干预是 warmup 强制夹爪闭合 — 让随机手臂运动至少有非零概率产生抓取事件。

insights: Reach is solved within 30 min single-env; manipulate is not — the bottleneck is grasping discovery, not reaching.

HER's effectiveness on FetchPickAndPlace is contingent on the cube moving at least sometimes. If the policy never grasps, HER degenerates into goal-near-cube-start relabeling and only teaches the reach component.
Auto-alpha with target_entropy=-action_dim=-4 collapses alpha from 1.0 to ~0.12 within 8K steps — exploration is gone well before any manipulation discovery is possible.

LayerNorm in actor under AUTO_ALPHA causes alpha to collapse even further (~0.002), because LN lets the squashed Gaussian satisfy the target entropy cheaply. LN also slows throughput ~30% on this small network.
Optimization-stability tweaks (LN-only) cannot rescue this run as long as exploration is dead; the bottleneck order is exploration > optimization.

CODEBASE CONVENTION: SACAgent.target_entropy is the NEGATIVE of H_target (used in alpha_loss = -log_alpha * (log_prob + target_entropy)). target_entropy = -4 means H_target = 4. To increase exploration, make target_entropy MORE negative (e.g., -8).
Without any exploration regularization (alpha near 0), the critic Q-values can diverge to ~50 — entropy bonus also acts as a soft constraint on Q-magnitude.

AUTO-ALPHA + DENSE REWARD INTERACTION: bigger Q magnitude from dense reward causes auto-alpha to drive alpha all the way to 0. So any reward-shape change implicitly retunes the exploration equilibrium. Pinning alpha (AUTO_ALPHA=False) decouples them.

实验 5 关键洞察:dense reward + 固定 alpha=0.2 让训练完全稳定(critic_loss ~0.12,alpha 全程 0.2,无发散),但成功率仍卡在 0.10 — 等于基线。证明优化质量和探索量都不是当前瓶颈。瓶颈是探索结构:i.i.d 高斯噪声在 4D 动作空间无法产生协调的多步抓取序列。要突破必须改变探索结构本身(强制夹爪闭合 / OU 噪声 / 多分量奖励塑形等)。
failed_directions: LayerNorm in BOTH actor + critic with AUTO_ALPHA on. Hurts reach, kills alpha, slows throughput. May still be worth trying critic-only LN once exploration is fixed.

Halving target_entropy magnitude (target_entropy = -action_dim/2). Counter-productive in this code's sign convention: lowers H_target, alpha collapses further, critic diverges. To go the other way, use -action_dim*2.
Dense reward ALONE (with AUTO_ALPHA still on). Alpha collapses to 0, deterministic policy never bumps cube, dense gradient is flat per episode. Need to pin alpha to make dense reward work.
Dense reward + 固定 alpha=0.2(AUTO_ALPHA=False)组合。训练干净稳定但成功率只到 0.10(等于基线)。问题不在信号或探索量,而在探索结构 — 单纯的 i.i.d 高斯噪声在 50 步 episode 内无法发现协调的抓取多步序列。
promising_directions: 下一步(实验 6):抓取偏置 warmup — 在 STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING 阶段强制 action[3]=-1(夹爪闭合),并把 warmup 提到 5000 步。直接攻击探索结构问题
OU 时间相关探索噪声(替代 i.i.d. 高斯,产生持续性夹爪指令)
多分量 dense reward:加 -lambda * ||grip-cube|| 项,引导夹爪贴近方块
TD3 替换 SAC(deterministic policy + 持久 action noise)
UTD > 1(在信号 + 探索结构问题修复后再考虑)
Critic-only LN(等探索结构修好再回头看)
